name: Nikolai Rimsky-Korsakov
abstract: Nikolai Andreyevich Rimsky-Korsakov (18 March 1844 – 21 June 1908) was a Russian composer, a member of the group of composers known as The Five. His best-known orchestral compositions—Capriccio Espagnol, the Russian Easter Festival Overture, and the symphonic suite Scheherazade—are staples of the classical music repertoire, along with su…

about_quotes.speaker: Diblasio
about_quotes.evidence: Rimsky Korsakov was Stravinsky’s teacher and the way it was explained to me was Stravinsky goes to Rimsky and says should I use the piano, should I not use the piano.

about_quotes.speaker: Howard Mandel
about_quotes.evidence: I liked the Russians. I liked the drama. Rimsky-Korsakov, anything with the rhythm and the exotic colorations.
about_quotes.doc_title: Howard Mandel interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 9, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Sauter
about_quotes.evidence: some of them were pupils of Rimsky-Korsakov, so I wanted that; but that didn't last very long.

about_quotes.speaker: Quincy Jones
about_quotes.evidence: equivalent to the three B’s, or to Rimsky-Korsakov, Shostakovich, and Stravinsky in Russia
about_quotes.doc_title: Quincy Jones — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Sam Wooding
about_quotes.evidence: Some Rimsky-Korsakov, and one that we had was the 'Overture of 1812'.

about_quotes.speaker: Hubert Laws
about_quotes.evidence: Rimsky-Korsakov, when he did Scheherazade. That was one of my first classical pieces I ever heard.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hubert Laws — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
